WNBA: Joint NBA+WNBA Board of Governors (NBA owns ~42% of WNBA)
NBA: NBA Board of Governors alone; 23 of 30 owners must approve
Key Difference: WNBA expansion is co-governed by the NBA, not fully independent
NBA fees are ~30-40x larger; WNBA fees growing far faster percentage-wise
WNBA more flexible on venue size, often shares an NBA arena

WNBA: Individuals, investor groups, or the local NBA owner
NBA: Controlling owner needs >=15% stake; PE capped at 20%/team, 30% combined
Key Difference: NBA has more codified ownership rules given far larger deal sizes

NBA: Expansion draft: teams historically protect up to 8
Key Difference: Similar mechanism; NBA lets incumbents protect more

WNBA: Many teams share ownership/arena with an NBA team (Valkyries/Warriors, Fever/Pacers)
NBA: N/A - NBA is the anchor franchise
Key Difference: WNBA expansion often piggybacks on existing NBA infrastructure

WNBA: Market viability, ownership commitment, fan/corporate/media support, community commitment to women's sports
NBA: Market revenue potential, arena readiness, ownership financial strength
Key Difference: WNBA weighs community commitment explicitly; NBA criteria are almost purely financial

WNBA: 2-4 years between award and first tip-off
Key Difference: Comparable lead times once a formal vote occurs
